title: Structured Observability, Health, Profiling, Capacity, and Incidents · Runtime Contract: Otel Server Spans
summary: Logs contain tokens and raw paths, metrics use user IDs, traces mark every 404 as a server error, readiness ignores database saturation, and a p50 benchmark drives release. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo otel server spans increment in low-cardinality service evidence and incident dashboard. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply current OpenTelemetry server span kind, low-cardinality route, method, status, error.type, network and server attributes, propagation, sampling, and 4xx semantics.
Measure duration distributions, active requests, request and response bytes, status classes, cancellations, rejects, saturation, and dependencies with bounded attributes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Otel Server Spans · Theory Otel Server Spans
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 7438 in a privacy-sensitive records API using conditional requests. Build deterministic Go evidence to use bounded route telemetry and distinguish server errors from readiness capacity; then replay the same operation key with different content and reject the mismatch. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Otel Server Spans contract for the supplied data.
